v1: AC goes completely out (with elderly people or young kids in house)
gas leak related to heating systems
v2: AC outages
office@swifthvac.com
sandra@swifthvac.com (for emergencies)

changes.v1: Tell them we have a technician on call and they will receive a call back within 20 minutes. And collect their name, number and address.
changes.v2: For non-emergencies after hours, collect their info and tell them we'll call back next business day. For emergencies, make it clear we take AC outages seriously especially in summer and be empathetic.
During hours, ask for their zip code to know if they're in our service area before booking anything.

v1: What is the office phone number for non-emergency calls?
What is the preferred order for emergency routing (e.g., call primary, then secondary; or call both simultaneously)?
What is the timeout duration for call transfers in seconds?
How many times should the system attempt to retry a call transfer?
What is the general call flow for calls received during business hours?
What message should be played if a call transfer fails (during business hours)?

What should the callback message say if the transfer fails?
